requirements.full_sentence:
1.1.1 These directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Frauds
classification and reporting by commercial banks and select FIs) directions
2016.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Frauds classification and reporting by commercial banks and select FIs) directions 2016

requirements.full_sentence:
1.1.2 These directions shall come into effect on the day they are placed on
the official website of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI).
requirements.object: the day they are placed on the official website of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI)

requirements.full_sentence:
1.2 Applicability
The provisions of these Directions shall apply to scheduled commercial banks
(excluding RRBs) and select FIs operating in India
requirements.object: banks (excluding RRBs) and select FIs operating in India

requirements.full_sentence:
These directions also aim to enable faster dissemination of
information by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to banks on the details of
frauds, unscrupulous borrowers and related parties, based on banks'
reporting so that necessary safeguards / preventive measures by way of
appropriate procedures and internal checks may be introduced and caution
exercised while dealing with such parties by banks.
requirements.object: exercised while dealing with such parties by banks

requirements.full_sentence:
2.1.1 The Chairmen and Managing Directors/Chief Executive Officers
(CMD/CEOs) of banks must provide focus on the "Fraud Prevention and
Management Function" to enable, among others, effective investigation of
fraud cases and prompt as well as accurate reporting to appropriate
regulatory and law enforcement authorities including Reserve Bank of India.
requirements.object: "Fraud Prevention and Management Function" to enable, among others, effective investigation of fraud cases and prompt as well as

requirements.full_sentence:
2.1.2 The fraud risk management, fraud monitoring and fraud investigation
function must be owned by the bank's CEO, Audit Committee of the Board
and the Special Committee of the Board.
requirements.object: bank's CEO, Audit Committee of the Board and the Special Committee of the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
2.1.3 Banks with the approval of their respective Boards, shall frame internal
policy for fraud risk management and fraud investigation function, based on
the governance standards relating to the ownership of the function and
accountability resting on defined and dedicated organizational set up and
operating processes.
requirements.object: fraud risk management and fraud investigation function, based on the governance standards relating to the ownership of the f

requirements.full_sentence:
2.1.4 Banks shall send the Fraud Monitoring Returns1 (FMR) through the
XBRL system, as detailed in para 3.2 below.
requirements.object: Returns1 (FMR) through the XBRL system, as detailed in para 3

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks should specifically
nominate an official of the rank of General Manager who will be responsible
for submitting all the returns referred to in this circular.
requirements.object: of the rank of General Manager who will be responsible for submitting all the returns referred to in this circular

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the above, the following cases shall be
treated as fraud and reported accordingly:
a.
requirements.object: and reported accordingly: a

requirements.full_sentence:
3.2.3 Fraud reports should also be submitted in cases where central
investigating agencies have initiated criminal proceedings suo moto and/or
where the Reserve Bank has directed that such cases be reported as frauds.
requirements.object: cases where central investigating agencies have initiated criminal proceedings suo moto and/or where the Reserve Bank has directe

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks may also report frauds perpetrated in their subsidiaries and
affiliates/joint ventures in FMR format in hard copy only.
requirements.object: in their subsidiaries and affiliates/joint ventures in FMR format in hard copy only

requirements.full_sentence:
3.2.5 Banks (other than foreign banks) having overseas branches/offices
should report all frauds perpetrated at such branches/offices also to RBI.
requirements.object: at such branches/offices also to RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
3.2.6 In addition to the FMR, banks are required to furnish a Flash Report
(FR) for frauds involving amounts of ₹50 million and above within a week of
such frauds coming to the notice of the bank's head office.
requirements.object: Report (FR) for frauds involving amounts of ₹50 million and above within a week of such frauds coming to the notice of the bank's h

requirements.full_sentence:
The FR,
inter alia, should include amount involved, nature of fraud, modus operandi in
brief, name of the branch/ office, names of parties involved, their constitution,
names of proprietors / partners and directors, names of officials involved and
lodging of complaint with police/CBI.
requirements.object: of fraud, modus operandi in brief, name of the branch/ office, names of parties involved, their constitution, names of

requirements.full_sentence:
As the complete particulars on frauds
perpetrated in the banks are vital for monitoring and supervisory purposes
and dissemination of information through Caution Advice / Central Fraud
Registry (CFR), banks should ensure that the data furnished are
complete/accurate and up-to-date.
requirements.object: furnished are complete/accurate and up-to-date

conditions:
if no data is to be provided in
respect of any of the items

if details of any of the items are not available at
the time of reporting of FMR return
requirements.full_sentence:
Incidentally, if no data is to be provided in
respect of any of the items, or if details of any of the items are not available at
the time of reporting of FMR return, the bank may indicate as "no particulars
to be reported" or "details not available at present" etc.
requirements.object: to be reported" or "details not available at present" etc

requirements.full_sentence:
All banks/Financial Institutions should furnish to Department of Banking
Supervision, Central Fraud Monitoring Cell, Bengaluru any changes in the
names of officials that will be necessary for inclusion in the Record.
requirements.object: Banking Supervision, Central Fraud Monitoring Cell, Bengaluru any changes in the names of officials that will be necessary fo

requirements.full_sentence:
3.3.1 Banks should ensure that the reporting system is suitably streamlined so
that delays in reporting of frauds, submission of delayed and incomplete fraud
reports are avoided.
requirements.object: system is suitably streamlined so that delays in reporting of frauds, submission of delayed and incomplete fraud reports are

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks must fix staff accountability in respect of delays in
reporting fraud cases to RBI.
requirements.object: respect of delays in reporting fraud cases to RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks should therefore, strictly adhere
to the timeframe fixed in this circular for reporting of fraud cases to RBI failing
which they would be liable for penal action prescribed under Section 47(A) of
the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
requirements.object: the timeframe fixed in this circular for reporting of fraud cases to RBI failing which they would be liable for penal ac

requirements.full_sentence:
4.1 Reports to the Board
Banks should ensure that all frauds of ₹0.1 million and above are reported to
their Boards promptly on their detection.
requirements.object: of ₹0

requirements.full_sentence:
Such reports should, among other
things, take note of the failure on the part of the concerned branch officials
and controlling authorities, and give details of action initiated against the
officials responsible for the fraud.
requirements.object: take note of the failure on the part of the concerned branch officials and controlling authorities, and give details of action i

requirements.full_sentence:
4.2.1 Information relating to frauds for the quarters ending June, September
and December shall be placed before the Audit Committee of the Board of
Directors during the month following the quarter to which it pertains.
requirements.object: Audit Committee of the Board of Directors during the month following the quarter to which it pertains

requirements.full_sentence:
4.2.2 These should be accompanied by supplementary material analysing
statistical information and details of each fraud so that the Audit Committee of
the Board would have adequate material to contribute effectively in regard to
the punitive or preventive aspects of frauds.
requirements.object: material analysing statistical information and details of each fraud so that the Audit Committee of the Board would ha

requirements.full_sentence:
4.3.1 Banks should conduct an annual review of the frauds and place a note
before the Board of Directors/Local Advisory Board for information.
requirements.object: of the frauds and place a note before the Board of Directors/Local Advisory Board for information

requirements.full_sentence:
The
reviews for the year-ended March shall be put up to the Board before the end
of the next quarter i.e.
requirements.object: the Board before the end of the next quarter i

requirements.full_sentence:
Such reviews need not be
sent to RBI but may be preserved for verification by the Reserve Bank's
inspecting officers.
requirements.object: by the Reserve Bank's inspecting officers

requirements.full_sentence:
4.3.2 The main aspects to be taken into account while making such a review
shall, inter alia, include the following:
requirements.object: the following:

requirements.full_sentence:
(e) Whether frauds are reported to local Police or CBI, as the case may
be, for investigation, as per the guidelines issued in this regard to
public sector banks by Government of India.
requirements.object: per the guidelines issued in this regard to public sector banks by Government of India

requirements.full_sentence:
4.3.3 The annual reviews should also, among other things, include the
following details:
requirements.object: include the following details:

requirements.full_sentence:
4.3.4 Banks shall place the copy of the circular on modus-operandi of fraud
issued by them for alerting their branches/controlling offices etc., on specific
frauds before the Audit Committee of Board (ACB) in its periodical meetings.
requirements.object: the circular on modus-operandi of fraud issued by them for alerting their branches/controlling offices etc

requirements.full_sentence:
4.4.1 While Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) shall monitor all the cases of
frauds in general, banks are required to constitute a Special Committee of the
Board for monitoring and follow up of cases of frauds (SCBF) involving
amounts of ₹10 million and above exclusively.
requirements.object: of frauds in general, banks are required to constitute a Special Committee of the Board for monitoring and follow up of cases of

requirements.full_sentence:
The periodicity of the meetings of the Special
Committee may be decided according to the number of cases involved.
requirements.object: the number of cases involved

requirements.full_sentence:
In
addition, the Committee should meet and review as and when a fraud
involving an amount of ₹10 million and above comes to light.
requirements.object: and when a fraud involving an amount of ₹10 million and above comes to light

requirements.full_sentence:
4.4.3 The banks shall delineate in a policy document the processes for
implementation of the Committee's directions and enable a dedicated outfit of
the bank to implement the directions in this regard.
requirements.object: document the processes for implementation of the Committee's directions and enable a dedicated outfit of the bank to implement t

requirements.full_sentence:
The detailed guidelines for private sector and foreign banks were issued on
May 26, 2011 to address all issues arising out of lapses in the functioning of
the private sector and foreign banks especially relating to corruption,
malpractices, frauds etc.
requirements.object: all issues arising out of lapses in the functioning of the private sector and foreign banks especially relating to corruption, mal

requirements.full_sentence:
However, banks should continue to place the
report on individual cases of attempted fraud involving an amount of ₹10
million and above before the Audit Committee of its Board.
requirements.object: report on individual cases of attempted fraud involving an amount of ₹10 million and above before the Audit Committee of its Boa

requirements.full_sentence:
The report should
cover the following viz.

as on March 31 every year should
be put up to the ACB within three months of the end of the relative year.
requirements.object: the ACB within three months of the end of the relative year

requirements.full_sentence:
5.1 Banks shall report to CFMC, RBI and the SSM (Senior Supervisory
Manager) of RBI, the details of fraud cases of ₹0.1 million and above closed
along with reasons for the closure after completing the process as given
below.
requirements.object: and the SSM (Senior Supervisory Manager) of RBI, the details of fraud cases of ₹0

requirements.full_sentence:
5.2 Banks should - close only such cases where the actions as stated
below are complete and prior approval is obtained from the SSM.
requirements.object: cases where the actions as stated below are complete and prior approval is obtained from the SSM

requirements.full_sentence:
5.3 Banks should also pursue vigorously with CBI for final disposal of
pending fraud cases especially where the banks have completed staff side
action.
requirements.object: CBI for final disposal of pending fraud cases especially where the banks have completed staff side action

requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly, banks may vigorously follow up with the police authorities
and/or court for final disposal of fraud cases.
requirements.object: the police authorities and/or court for final disposal of fraud cases

requirements.full_sentence:
5.5 The banks are required to follow the guidelines relating to seeking prior
approval for closure of such cases from the SSM and follow up of such cases
after closure as mentioned below.
requirements.object: relating to seeking prior approval for closure of such cases from the SSM and follow up of such cases after closure as mentio

requirements.full_sentence:
5.6 The banks shall have to submit their proposals, case wise, for closure
to the SSM of the bank.
requirements.object: proposals, case wise, for closure to the SSM of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The cases may be closed after getting the approval of
the SSM.
requirements.object: the approval of the SSM

requirements.full_sentence:
The banks should maintain the record of details of such cases in a
separate ledger.
requirements.object: details of such cases in a separate ledger

requirements.full_sentence:
Even after closure of the fraud cases for limited statistical
purposes, banks should vigorously follow up with the investigating agencies
(CBI / Police) to ensure that the investigation process is taken to its logical
conclusion.
requirements.object: the investigating agencies (CBI / Police) to ensure that the investigation process is taken to its logical conclusion

requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly, the banks should continue to ensure that they are
regularly and appropriately represented in the court proceedings as and when
required.
requirements.object: they are regularly and appropriately represented in the court proceedings as and when required

requirements.full_sentence:
All the relevant records pertaining to such cases must be preserved
till the cases are finally disposed of by CBI / Police or Courts, as the case may
be.
requirements.object: cases are finally disposed of by CBI / Police or Courts, as the case may be

requirements.full_sentence:
5.7 The banks shall, with the approval of their respective Boards, frame
their own internal policy for closure of fraud cases, incorporating the above
norms and other internal procedures / controls as deemed necessary.
requirements.object: of their respective Boards, frame their own internal policy for closure of fraud cases, incorporating the above norms and other in

conditions:
when Police / CBI investigation is in progress or cases are pending in the
court of law
requirements.full_sentence:
5.8 Notwithstanding the fact that banks may close cases of fraud even
when Police / CBI investigation is in progress or cases are pending in the
court of law, they should complete, within the prescribed time frame, the
process of examination of staff accountability or conclude staff side actions.
requirements.object: even when Police / CBI investigation is in progress or cases are pending in the court of law, they should complete, within the pr

requirements.full_sentence:
6.1 In dealing with cases of fraud/embezzlement, banks should not merely be
actuated by the necessity of recovering expeditiously the amount involved, but
should also be motivated by public interest and the need for ensuring that the
guilty persons do not go unpunished.
requirements.object: the necessity of recovering expeditiously the amount involved, but should also be motivated by public interest and the need for

requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, as a general rule, the
following cases should invariably be referred to the State Police or to the CBI
as detailed below:
Category of
Amount involved
Agency to whom complaint should
Remarks
bank
in the fraud
be lodged
Private Sector/ Foreign Banks
₹10000 and above
State Police If committed by staff
₹0.1 million and above
State Police If committed by outsiders on their own and/or with the connivance of bank staff/officers.
requirements.object: the State Police or to the CBI as detailed below: Category of Amount involved Agency to whom complaint should Remarks bank i

requirements.full_sentence:
6.2 All fraud cases of value below ₹10,000/- involving bank officials, should be
referred to the Regional Head of the bank, who would scrutinize each case and
direct the bank branch concerned on whether it should be reported to the local police
station for further legal action.
requirements.object: Regional Head of the bank, who would scrutinize each case and direct the bank branch concerned on whether it should be reported to

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks
were also given an illustrative list of some of the preventive measures they
may follow in this regard viz.
requirements.object: viz

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks shall reduce or increase the threshold limits mentioned above with the
approval of the Board depending on the volume of cheques handled by the
bank or it's risk appetite.
requirements.object: threshold limits mentioned above with the approval of the Board depending on the volume of cheques handled by the bank or it's

requirements.full_sentence:
7.2 Banks may also consider the following preventive measures for dealing
with suspicious or large value cheques (in relation to an account's normal
level of operations):
requirements.object: preventive measures for dealing with suspicious or large value cheques (in relation to an account's normal level of operat

requirements.full_sentence:
The above may be resorted to selectively if not found feasible to be
implemented systematically.
requirements.object: if not found feasible to be implemented systematically

requirements.full_sentence:
In such cases the presenting bank shall
immediately hand over the underlying instrument to drawee/paying bank as
and when demanded to enable it to file an FIR with the police authorities and
report the fraud to RBI.
requirements.object: underlying instrument to drawee/paying bank as and when demanded to enable it to file an FIR with the police authorities and

requirements.full_sentence:
7.5 However, in the case of collection of an instrument which is genuine but
the amount is collected fraudulently by a person who is not the true owner or
where the amount has been credited before realisation and subsequently the
instrument is found to be fake/forged and returned by the paying bank, the
collecting bank, which is defrauded or is at loss by paying the amount before
realisation of the instrument, shall file both the fraud report with the RBI and
complaint with the police.
requirements.object: report with the RBI and complaint with the police

conditions:
In case of collection of altered/fake cheque involving two or more
branches of the same bank

where the altered/fake cheque has
been encashed
requirements.full_sentence:
7.6 In case of collection of altered/fake cheque involving two or more
branches of the same bank, the branch where the altered/fake cheque has
been encashed, should report the fraud to its Head Office.
requirements.object: its Head Office

requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly in the
event of an altered/fake cheque having been paid / encashed involving two or
more branches of a bank under Core Banking Solution (CBS), the branch
which has released the payment should report the fraud to the Head Office.
requirements.object: the Head Office

requirements.full_sentence:
Loan Frauds - New Framework
Based on the recommendations of an Internal Working Group constituted by
the Bank , a framework for dealing with loan frauds was put in place vide
circular DBS.CO.CFMC.BC.No.007/23.04.001/2014-15 dated May 7, 2015.

The early
detection of Fraud and the necessary corrective action are important to
reduce the quantum of loss which the continuance of the Fraud may entail.

These signals in a loan account should immediately put the bank on
alert regarding a weakness or wrong doing which may ultimately turn out to be
fraudulent.
requirements.object: be fraudulent

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank cannot afford to ignore such EWS but must instead use
them as a trigger to launch a detailed investigation into a RFA.
requirements.object: a trigger to launch a detailed investigation into a RFA

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks may choose to adopt or adapt the relevant
signals from this list and also include other alerts/signals based on their
experience, client profile and business models.
requirements.object: adapt the relevant signals from this list and also include other alerts/signals based on their experience, client profile and busin

requirements.full_sentence:
All accounts beyond ₹500 million
classified as RFA or 'Frauds' must also be reported on the CRILC data
platform together with the dates on which the accounts were classified as
such.
requirements.object: the CRILC data platform together with the dates on which the accounts were classified as such

requirements.full_sentence:
However, banks shall continue to
report all identified accounts to CFMC, RBI as per the existing cut-offs.
requirements.object: identified accounts to CFMC, RBI as per the existing cut-offs

requirements.full_sentence:
8.3.5 The tracking of EWS in loan accounts should not be seen as an
additional task but must be integrated with the credit monitoring process in the
bank so that it becomes a continuous activity and also acts as a trigger for
any possible credit impairment in the loan accounts, given the interplay
between credit risks and fraud risks.
requirements.object: additional task but must be integrated with the credit monitoring process in the bank so that it becomes a continuous activity and also

requirements.full_sentence:
The officer
responsible for the operations in the account, by whatever designation called,
should be sensitised to observe and report any manifestation of the EWS
promptly to the Fraud Monitoring Group (FMG) or any other group constituted
by the bank for the purpose immediately.
requirements.object: and report any manifestation of the EWS promptly to the Fraud Monitoring Group (FMG) or any other group constituted by the ba

requirements.full_sentence:
To ensure that the exercise remains
meaningful, such officers may be held responsible for non-reporting or delays
in reporting.
requirements.object: non-reporting or delays in reporting

requirements.full_sentence:
8.3.6 The FMG or any such designated committee shall classify the account
as RFA and the details of RFA accounts shall be put up to the CMD/CEO
every month.
requirements.object: RFA and the details of RFA accounts shall be put up to the CMD/CEO every month

requirements.full_sentence:
8.3.7 A report on the RFA accounts shall be put up to the Special Committee
of the Board for monitoring and follow-up of Frauds (SCBF) providing, inter
alia, a synopsis of the remedial action taken together with their current status.
requirements.object: the Special Committee of the Board for monitoring and follow-up of Frauds (SCBF) providing, inter alia, a synopsis of the remedial action

requirements.full_sentence:
Among other things, delays in reporting of frauds also delays
the alerting of other banks about the modus operandi through Caution Advice
/ CFR by RBI that may result in similar frauds being perpetrated elsewhere.
requirements.object: being perpetrated elsewhere

requirements.full_sentence:
Any weakness that
may have escaped attention at the appraisal stage can often be mitigated in
case the post disbursement monitoring remains effective.
requirements.object: the appraisal stage can often be mitigated in case the post disbursement monitoring remains effective

conditions:
if any, strictures
passed against them by Government agencies
requirements.full_sentence:
In order to
strengthen the monitoring processes, based on an analysis of the collective
experience of the banks, inclusion of the following checks / investigations
during the different stages of the loan life-cycle should be carried out:
a) Pre-sanction: As part of the credit process, the checks being applied
during the stage of pre-sanction may consist of the Risk Management
Group (RMG) or any other appropriate group of the bank collecting
independent information and market intelligence on the potential
borrowers from the public domain on their track record, involvement in
legal disputes, raids conducted on their businesses, if any, strictures
passed against them by Government agencies, validation of submitted
information/data from other sources like the ROC, gleaning from the
defaulters list of RBI/other Government agencies, etc., which could be
used as an input by the sanctioning authority.
requirements.object: Management Group (RMG) or any other appropriate group of the bank collecting independent information and market intelligence on the potential borrowers from the public domain on their track record, involvement in legal disputes, raids conducted on their businesses

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks shall keep the
record of such pre-sanction checks as part of the sanction
documentation.
requirements.object: such pre-sanction checks as part of the sanction documentation

requirements.full_sentence:
b) Disbursement: Checks by RMG during the disbursement stage, shall
among others, focus on the adherence to the terms and conditions of
sanction, rationale for allowing dilution of these terms and conditions,
level at which such dilutions were allowed, etc.
requirements.object: the adherence to the terms and conditions of sanction, rationale for allowing dilution of these terms and conditions, level at

requirements.full_sentence:
The dilutions should
strictly conform to the broad framework laid down by the Board in this
regard.
requirements.object: broad framework laid down by the Board in this regard

requirements.full_sentence:
As a matter of good practice, the sanctioning authority may
specify certain terms and conditions as 'core' which should not be
diluted.
requirements.object: conditions as 'core' which should not be diluted

requirements.full_sentence:
The RMG may immediately flag the non-adherence of core
stipulations to the sanctioning authority.
requirements.object: of core stipulations to the sanctioning authority

requirements.full_sentence:
Among
other things, the aspects of diversion of funds in an account, adequacy
of stock vis-a-vis stock statements, stress in group accounts, etc., must
also be commented upon at the time of review.
requirements.object: at the time of review

requirements.full_sentence:
Besides, the RMG
should have capability to track market developments relating to the
major clients of the bank and provide inputs to the credit officers.
requirements.object: market developments relating to the major clients of the bank and provide inputs to the credit officers

requirements.full_sentence:
8.5 Staff empowerment
Employees should be encouraged to report fraudulent activity in an account,
along with the reasons in support of their views, to the appropriately
constituted authority, under the Whistle Blower Policy of the bank, who may
institute a scrutiny through the FMG.
requirements.object: the FMG

requirements.full_sentence:
The FMG may 'hear' the concerned
employee in order to obtain necessary clarifications.
requirements.object: in order to obtain necessary clarifications

requirements.full_sentence:
Protection should be
available to such employees under the whistle blower policy of the bank so
that the fear of victimisation does not act as a deterrent.
requirements.object: employees under the whistle blower policy of the bank so that the fear of victimisation does not act as a deterrent

requirements.full_sentence:
8.6 Role of Auditors
During the course of the audit, auditors may come across instances where the
transactions in the account or the documents point to the possibility of
fraudulent transactions in the account.
requirements.object: the transactions in the account or the documents point to the possibility of fraudulent transactions in the account

requirements.full_sentence:
In such a situation, the auditor should
immediately bring it to the notice of the top management and if necessary to
the Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) for appropriate action.
requirements.object: the notice of the top management and if necessary to the Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) for appropriate action

conditions: In case of accounts classified as 'fraud'
requirements.full_sentence:
8.7 Incentive for Prompt Reporting
In case of accounts classified as 'fraud', banks are required to make
provisions to the full extent immediately, irrespective of the value of security.
requirements.object: the full extent immediately, irrespective of the value of security

conditions: in case a bank is unable to make the entire provision in one go
requirements.full_sentence:
However, in case a bank is unable to make the entire provision in one go, it
may now do so over four quarters provided there is no delay in reporting (cf.
requirements.object: four quarters provided there is no delay in reporting (cf

requirements.full_sentence:
In case of delays, the banks
under Multiple Banking Arrangements (MBA) or member banks in the
consortium are required to make the provision in one go in terms of the said
circular.
requirements.object: in one go in terms of the said circular

conditions: where the bank is the sole lender
requirements.full_sentence:
8.8.1 In cases where the bank is the sole lender, the FMG will take a call on
whether an account in which EWS are observed should be classified as RFA
or not.
requirements.object: or not

requirements.full_sentence:
This exercise should be completed as soon as possible and in any
case within a month of the EWS being noticed.
requirements.object: as possible and in any case within a month of the EWS being noticed

requirements.full_sentence:
8.8.2 The bank may use external auditors, including forensic experts or an
internal team for investigations before taking a final view on the RFA.
requirements.object: forensic experts or an internal team for investigations before taking a final view on the RFA

requirements.full_sentence:
At the
end of this time line, which cannot be more than six months, banks should
either lift the RFA status or classify the account as a fraud.
requirements.object: status or classify the account as a fraud

requirements.full_sentence:
8.8.3 A report on the RFA accounts should be put up to the SCBF with the
observations/decision of the FMG.
requirements.object: the SCBF with the observations/decision of the FMG

requirements.full_sentence:
The report should list the EWS/
irregularities observed in the account and provide a synopsis of the
investigations ordered / remedial action proposed by the FMG together with
their current status.
requirements.object: observed in the account and provide a synopsis of the investigations ordered / remedial action proposed by the FMG togeth

requirements.full_sentence:
8.9.2 In view of this, all the banks which have financed a borrower under
'multiple banking' arrangement should take co-ordinated action, based on
commonly agreed strategy, for legal / criminal actions, follow up for recovery,
exchange of details on modus operandi, achieving consistency in data /
information on frauds reported to Reserve Bank of India.
requirements.object: on commonly agreed strategy, for legal / criminal actions, follow up for recovery, exchange of details on modus operan

requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, the bank
which detects a fraud is required to immediately share the details with all
other banks in the multiple banking arrangements.
requirements.object: details with all other banks in the multiple banking arrangements

requirements.full_sentence:
8.9.3 In case of consortium arrangements, individual banks must conduct
their own due diligence before taking any credit exposure and also
independently monitor the end use of funds rather than depend fully on the
consortium leader.
requirements.object: diligence before taking any credit exposure and also independently monitor the end use of funds rather than depend fully on the

requirements.full_sentence:
However, as regards monitoring of Escrow Accounts, the
details may be worked out by the consortium and duly documented so that
accountability can be fixed easily at a later stage.
requirements.object: the consortium and duly documented so that accountability can be fixed easily at a later stage

requirements.full_sentence:
Besides, any major
concerns from the fraud perspective noticed at the time of annual reviews or
through the tracking of early warning signals should be shared with other
consortium / multiple banking lenders immediately as hitherto.
requirements.object: consortium / multiple banking lenders immediately as hitherto

conditions:
In case it is decided at the individual
bank level to classify the account as fraud straightaway at this stage itself
requirements.timeline:
within 21 days of detection and also
report the case to CBI
requirements.full_sentence:
In case it is decided at the individual
bank level to classify the account as fraud straightaway at this stage itself, the
bank shall then report the fraud to RBI within 21 days of detection and also
report the case to CBI/Police, as is being done hitherto.
requirements.object: to RBI within 21 days of detection and also report the case to CBI/Police, as is being done hitherto

requirements.timeline:
within 15 days of such
a request being received
requirements.full_sentence:
The
meeting of the JLF so requisitioned must be convened within 15 days of such
a request being received.
requirements.object: days of such a request being received

conditions: In case there is a broad agreement
requirements.full_sentence:
In case there is a broad agreement, the account
should be classified as a fraud;
requirements.object: fraud

requirements.full_sentence:
else based on the majority rule of agreement
amongst banks with at least 60% share in the total lending, the account
should be red flagged by all the banks and subjected to a forensic audit
commissioned or initiated by the consortium leader or the largest lender under
MBA.
requirements.object: all the banks and subjected to a forensic audit commissioned or initiated by the consortium leader or the largest lender under MBA

requirements.full_sentence:
All banks, as part of the consortium or multiple banking arrangement,
shall share the costs and provide the necessary support for such an
investigation.
requirements.object: provide the necessary support for such an investigation

requirements.full_sentence:
8.9.5 The forensic audit must be completed within a maximum period of
three months from the date of the JLF meeting authorizing the audit.
requirements.object: maximum period of three months from the date of the JLF meeting authorizing the audit

requirements.timeline:
Within 15
days of the completion of the forensic audit
requirements.full_sentence:
Within 15
days of the completion of the forensic audit, the JLF shall reconvene and
decide on the status of the account, either by consensus or the majority rule
as specified above.
requirements.object: the status of the account, either by consensus or the majority rule as specified above

conditions: In case the decision is to classify the account as a fraud
requirements.full_sentence:
In case the decision is to classify the account as a fraud,
the RFA status shall be changed to Fraud in all banks and reported to RBI
and on the CRILC platform within a week of the said decision.
requirements.object: in all banks and reported to RBI and on the CRILC platform within a week of the said decision

requirements.full_sentence:
Besides, within
30 days of the RBI reporting, the bank commissioning/ initiating the forensic
audit should lodge a complaint with the CBI on behalf of all banks in the
consortium/MBA.
requirements.object: the CBI on behalf of all banks in the consortium/MBA

conditions:
if the bank initiating the forensic audit is a
private sector bank
requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, if the bank initiating the forensic audit is a
private sector bank, the complaint shall be lodged with the CBI by the PSU
bank with the largest exposure to the account in the consortium/MBA.
requirements.object: CBI by the PSU bank with the largest exposure to the account in the consortium/MBA

conditions:
If there
is no PSU bank in the consortium / MBA or it is a solo bank lending by a
private sector bank/foreign bank
requirements.full_sentence:
If there
is no PSU bank in the consortium / MBA or it is a solo bank lending by a
private sector bank/foreign bank, the private bank/foreign bank shall report to
the Police as per extant instructions.
requirements.object: as per extant instructions

requirements.full_sentence:
8.9.6 It may be noted that the overall time allowed for the entire exercise to
be completed is six months from the date when the first member bank
reported the account as RFA or Fraud on the CRILC platform.
requirements.object: overall time allowed for the entire exercise to be completed is six months from the date when the first member bank reported the acc

requirements.full_sentence:
8.10.1 As in the case of accounts categorised as NPAs, banks must initiate
and complete a staff accountability exercise within six months from the date of
classification as a Fraud.
requirements.object: staff accountability exercise within six months from the date of classification as a Fraud

requirements.full_sentence:
Wherever felt necessary or warranted, the role of
sanctioning official(s) may also be covered under this exercise.
requirements.object: this exercise

requirements.full_sentence:
The
completion of the staff accountability exercise for frauds and the action taken
shall be placed before the SCBF and intimated to the RBI through the FMR
Update Application supplied to them.
requirements.object: SCBF and intimated to the RBI through the FMR Update Application supplied to them

requirements.full_sentence: 8.10.2 Banks may bifurcate all fraud cases into vigilance and non-vigilance.
requirements.object: into vigilance and non-vigilance

requirements.full_sentence: Only vigilance cases should be referred to the investigative authorities.
requirements.object: investigative authorities

requirements.full_sentence:
Non-
vigilance cases may be investigated and dealt with at the bank level within a
period of six months.
requirements.object: with at the bank level within a period of six months

requirements.full_sentence:
It is emphasised that banks should strive to complete
the staff accountability exercise within six months as clearing the air on the
staff members concerned in a shorter time frame is appropriate and desirable.
requirements.object: staff accountability exercise within six months as clearing the air on the staff members concerned in a shorter time frame is a

requirements.full_sentence:
8.10.3 In cases involving very senior executives of the bank, the Board / ACB/
SCBF may initiate the process of fixing staff accountability.
requirements.object: fixing staff accountability

requirements.full_sentence:
8.10.4 Staff accountability should not be held up on account of the case being
filed with law enforcement agencies.
requirements.object: account of the case being filed with law enforcement agencies

requirements.full_sentence:
Both the criminal and domestic enquiry
should be conducted simultaneously.

8.11.1 Banks are required to lodge the complaint with the law enforcement
agencies immediately on detection of fraud.
requirements.object: with the law enforcement agencies immediately on detection of fraud

requirements.full_sentence:
There should ideally not be any
delay in filing of the complaints with the law enforcement agencies since
delays may result in the loss of relevant 'relied upon' documents, non-
availability of witnesses, absconding of borrowers and also the money trail
getting cold in addition to asset stripping by the fraudulent borrower.
requirements.object: of relevant 'relied upon' documents, non- availability of witnesses, absconding of borrowers and also the money trail getting cold

requirements.full_sentence:
8.11.3 The complaint lodged by the bank with the law enforcement agencies
should be drafted properly and invariably be vetted by a legal officer.
requirements.object: invariably be vetted by a legal officer

requirements.full_sentence:
Since such grounds automatically constitute the
basis for classifying an account as a fraudulent one, banks should invariably
classify such accounts as frauds and report the same to RBI.
requirements.object: as frauds and report the same to RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
8.12.2 No restructuring or grant of additional facilities may be made in the
case of RFA or fraud accounts.
requirements.object: case of RFA or fraud accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
However, in cases of fraud/malfeasance
where the existing promoters are replaced by new promoters and the
borrower company is totally delinked from such erstwhile
promoters/management, banks and JLF may take a view on restructuring of
such accounts based on their viability, without prejudice to the continuance of
criminal action against the erstwhile promoters/management.
requirements.object: restructuring of such accounts based on their viability, without prejudice to the continuance of criminal action against the erstwhile

requirements.full_sentence:
In this regard the banks should follow normal
procedures and the processes followed should be suitably recorded.
requirements.object: the processes followed should be suitably recorded

requirements.full_sentence:
9.1.1 Banks should subject the title deeds and other documents in respect of
all credit exposures of `5 crore and above to periodic legal audit and re-
verification of title deeds with relevant authorities as part of regular audit
exercise till the loan stands fully repaid.
requirements.object: and other documents in respect of all credit exposures of `5 crore and above to periodic legal audit and re- verification of t

requirements.full_sentence:
9.1.2 Banks shall furnish a review note to their Board / Audit Committee of
the Board at quarterly intervals on an ongoing basis giving therein the
information in respect of such legal audits which should cover aspects, inter
alia, like number of loan accounts due for legal audit for the quarter, how
many accounts covered, list of deficiencies observed by the auditors, steps
taken to rectify the deficiencies, number of accounts in which the rectification
could not take place, course of action to safeguard the interest of bank in such
cases, action taken on issues pending from earlier quarters.
requirements.object: to their Board / Audit Committee of the Board at quarterly intervals on an ongoing basis giving therein the information in respe

requirements.full_sentence:
9.2.2 It is therefore advised that banks should ensure that while packaging
and selling performing or non-performing assets, it is properly ascertained that
the pool of assets being sold does not contain any loan originated fraudulently
or has been classified as fraud as on the date of sale.
requirements.object: and selling performing or non-performing assets, it is properly ascertained that the pool of assets being sold does not co
fax / e-mail instances of bank robberies, dacoities, thefts and burglaries to the following authorities immediately on their occu

requirements.full_sentence: The report should include following details;

10.2 Banks should also submit a quarterly Return3 on Bank Robberies,
Dacoities, Thefts and Burglaries to RBI electronically using the Application
3 The format is available on RBI website (https://rbi.org.in/scripts/Non_OSMOS.aspx)
supplied in XBRL system covering all cases as reported as per para 10.1
above during the quarter.
requirements.object: Return3 on Bank Robberies, Dacoities, Thefts and Burglaries to RBI electronically using the Application 3 The format is availa

requirements.timeline:
within 15 days of the end of
the quarter to which it relates
requirements.full_sentence:
This may be submitted within 15 days of the end of
the quarter to which it relates.
requirements.object: days of the end of the quarter to which it relates

requirements.full_sentence:
ANNEX -II
Early Warning Signals (EWS)
Some Early Warning Signals (EWS) which should alert the bank officials about
some wrongdoings in the loan accounts which may turn out to be fraudulent
1 a) Default in undisputed payment to the statutory bodies as declared in the
Annual report.
requirements.object: fraudulent 1 a) Default in undisputed payment to the statutory bodies as declared in the Annual report

requirements.full_sentence:
Circular Date Subject
